data.title: Polymarket strikes prediction market deal with major US soccer league

source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/polymarket-prediction-market-deal-us-major-league-soccer?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Matrixport: Central banks' continued gold purchases may explain the strength of gold and the relative pressure on Bitcoin.
data.description: Matrixport's chart analysis points out that the continued rise in gold prices is primarily driven by renewed concerns about the weakening purchasing power of the US dollar. Against the backdrop of Trump's renewed push for tariffs on Europe, the dollar is under pressure, while discussions about foreign central banks potentially reducing their holdings of US Treasury bonds and shifting more foreign exchange reserves to gold have increased significantly. The analysis suggests that gold's relative strength is closely related to official sector demand. Central banks worldwide are continuously increasing their gold holdings, with the People's Bank of China (PBoC) being particularly closely watched, providing sustained buying support for gold prices. In contrast, Bitcoin is still less commonly included in central bank reserve diversification frameworks in terms of public disclosure. For policymakers, gold remains a more mainstream asset and more in line with existing reserve management systems, while Bitcoin has not yet been widely accepted in official foreign exchange reserves. Matrixport suggests that this divergence in central bank asset allocation may, to some extent, explain the recent strength of gold and the relative weakness of Bitcoin.

data.title: Tokenization's Role in Global Finance Infrastructure Expands
data.description: Tokenization is transitioning from a speculative idea to a crucial element in global financial infrastructure, enhancing settlement speed, transparency, and operational efficiency. According to NS3.AI, while mainstream Western markets face perception challenges, emerging economies are utilizing tokenization to address legacy inefficiencies. Regulatory strategies differ worldwide, with Europe emphasizing formalization and clarity through the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework, while Gulf nations are implementing phased, supervised rollouts.

data.title: Solana Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow on January 26
data.description:
On January 27, Solana spot ETF experienced a net inflow of $2.46 million on January 26, according to PANews. The Bitwise SOL ETF BSOL was the sole contributor to this inflow, marking a single-day net inflow of $2.46 million and bringing its historical total net inflow to $684 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETF stands at $1.05 billion, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.50%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $876 million.

data.title: U.S. Data Center Construction Surges Amid AI Infrastructure Demand
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. The construction of data centers in the United States has significantly increased over the past three years, reflecting a shift in capital expenditure towards AI infrastructure rather than traditional office spaces. This trend indicates a change in economic expansion drivers, with data centers now serving as the backbone of growth instead of office buildings. The prosperity of regions is increasingly determined by factors such as electricity, grid connectivity, cooling systems, and fiber optics, which dictate the capacity for computational power.

The expansion of data centers highlights a fundamental shift where electricity is treated as a raw material and computational power as the product. The rise in data center construction is a visible outcome, but the underlying factor is the growing competition for grid resources. Looking ahead to 2026, there is optimism about the prospects for U.S. electricity stocks.

data.title: Bitcoin-Related Job Opportunities Expected to Rise by 2025
data.description:
Bitcoin-related job opportunities are projected to increase by 6% in 2025, reaching a total of 1,801 positions, according to PANews. The report, released by Bitvocation, highlights a significant rise in non-technical roles, which will account for 74% of all job openings, up from 69% in 2024. The most sought-after non-technical positions include product managers, executive assistants, marketing managers, directors, and product designers. Notably, director roles are expected to grow tenfold, indicating an expansion in operations among Bitcoin companies.

The report also reveals that over 150 companies focused solely on Bitcoin are actively hiring, with Riot Platforms and Lightspark leading the recruitment efforts. The top ten companies have seen a 122% increase in hiring compared to the previous year. The United States remains the leader with approximately 500 positions, while Singapore has experienced a remarkable 158% year-on-year growth, securing the second spot.

Remote Bitcoin positions have decreased from 53% to 45%, reflecting a shift in hiring trends within the industry.

data.title: DXY Decline Could Boost Cryptocurrency Market
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. The Dollar Index (DXY) has recently lost its support level, leading to a decline. This development is seen as a potential tailwind for the cryptocurrency market, particularly benefiting Ethereum (ETH) and alternative cryptocurrencies (alts). Historically, a weak DXY often precedes positive movements in the crypto market before prices react.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $7.76 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $7.76 million yesterday (January 26th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday (January 26th, Eastern Time) was the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP), with a single-day net inflow of $5.31 million and a historical total net inflow of $324 million. This was followed by the Canary XRP ETF (XRPC), with a single-day net inflow of $1.41 million and a historical total net inflow of $399 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $1.36 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.18%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.24 billion.

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw a net inflow of $2.46 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the Solana spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $2.46 million yesterday (January 26th, Eastern Time). Only the Bitwise SOL ETF (BSOL) saw net inflows yesterday (January 26th, Eastern Time), with a single-day net inflow of $2.46 million and a historical total net inflow of $684 million. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF has a total net asset value of $1.05 billion, a net asset value ratio of 1.50%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $876 million.

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows on January 26
data.description:
On January 27, it was reported that XRP spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $7.76 million on January 26, according to PANews. The Bitwise XRP ETF led the inflows with $5.31 million in a single day, bringing its historical total net inflow to $324 million. Following this, the Canary XRP ETF recorded a daily net inflow of $1.41 million, with a historical total net inflow reaching $399 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs stood at $1.36 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.18%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $1.24 billion.

data.title: Solana Chinese Community's Solar Account on X Hacked
data.description:
The official X account of the Solana Chinese community organization, Solar, has been hacked. According to Foresight News, the team is urgently working with the platform to resolve the issue, but the timeline for recovery is currently uncertain.

Users are advised to be cautious of any requests for information, funds, or sensitive actions made in the name of Solar on X, as these are scams. All official announcements and updates will continue to be shared through Telegram groups and other official channels, and not via direct messages on X. Users are urged not to click on suspicious links or disclose personal information.

data.title: South Korea's Trade Envoy to Visit U.S. Amid Tariff Agreement Discussions
data.description:
South Korea's presidential office announced on January 27 that the country's trade envoy will soon visit the United States to meet with the U.S. Trade Representative to discuss the implementation of trade agreements. According to BlockBeats, South Korea aims to express its commitment to enforcing tariff agreements during these discussions.

Earlier today, U.S. President Donald Trump announced an increase in tariffs on South Korean goods, stating, "The South Korean National Assembly has not fulfilled the agreement reached with the United States. I, along with South Korean President Lee Jae-myung, reached a mutually beneficial agreement on July 30, 2025, and reaffirmed these terms during my visit to South Korea on October 29, 2025. Why has the South Korean National Assembly not yet approved this agreement? Since the South Korean National Assembly has not passed our historic trade agreement, which is within their power, I hereby announce an increase in reciprocal tariffs on South Korean automobiles, timber, pharmaceuticals, and all other goods from 15% to 25%."

data.title: Crypto Traders and Investors Show Decreased Social Media Activity in January
data.description: Santiment posted on X that there is a noticeable decline in activity among crypto traders and investors. According to social data from various crypto social media platforms, the focus in January has shifted, indicating a potential decrease in engagement within the crypto community. This trend suggests that participants may be less active or have temporarily stepped back from the market during this period.

data.title: South Korea is considering allowing domestic institutions to issue virtual assets, but stablecoins remain controversial.
data.description: Speaking at the Asian Financial Forum in Hong Kong, Lee Chang-yong stated that due to market pressure, South Korean authorities have allowed residents to invest in overseas-issued virtual assets, and financial regulators are considering establishing a new registration system to allow domestic institutions to issue virtual assets. Lee pointed out that if a won-denominated stablecoin is launched, its primary use will likely be in cross-border transactions, while tokenized deposits are more suitable for domestic payment scenarios. However, he emphasized that significant controversy remains surrounding stablecoins. His core concern is whether won-denominated stablecoins could be used to circumvent capital flow management, especially when combined with dollar-denominated stablecoins. He further stated that dollar-denominated stablecoins have a wide range of applications, low barriers to entry, and significantly lower transaction costs than direct use of the dollar. When exchange rate fluctuations cause changes in market expectations, funds could rapidly flow into dollar-denominated stablecoins, resulting in large-scale capital transfers; simultaneously, the participation of numerous non-bank institutions in stablecoin issuance significantly increases regulatory difficulty. Furthermore, Lee pointed out that South Korea already possesses a highly developed fast payment system, thus limiting the advantages of retail central bank digital currencies (CBDCs). Currently, the central bank is simultaneously advancing tokenized deposits and wholesale CBDCs through multiple pilot programs in order to maintain the existing two-tier financial system.

data.title: Solar's official X account appears to have been hacked; users are advised to be wary of related scams.
data.description: The Solar community has issued a security alert stating that the official Solar X account is highly suspected of being hacked and is temporarily inaccessible. The team is urgently contacting the platform to resolve the issue, and the recovery time is yet to be determined. The official statement emphasizes that during this period, any messages claiming to be from Solar on the X platform requesting funds, sending private messages, or involving sensitive operations are scams. All official announcements and updates will only be released through the official Solar Telegram community and other established official channels (such as the official Solar X account), and no communication will be conducted with users via X private messages. Users are also reminded not to click on suspicious links, not to disclose personal information, and to contact community administrators immediately if they discover any unusual activity.

data.title: New AI Agent ‘Clawdbot’ Exposes Users to Remote Hijacking
data.description:
Security researchers warn that rapid adoption of the open-source automation tool, Clawdbot, has left thousands of servers vulnerable to shell access and API key theft. The post New AI Agent ‘Clawdbot’ Exposes Users to Remote Hijacking first appeared on The Shib Daily.
source: https://news.shib.io/2026/01/27/new-ai-agent-clawdbot-exposes-users-to-remote-hijacking/

data.title: Base Co-Founder Clarifies Stance on Market Manipulation Concerns
data.description: Base co-founder Jesse Pollak addressed community concerns regarding market manipulation in a recent tweet. According to Foresight News, Pollak responded to suggestions that Base should support a specific community to influence price trends, stating that the core team will not secretly manipulate the market. He emphasized that coordinating funds to artificially inflate asset prices would harm other assets, is unsustainable, contradicts the values of a free and open market, and could potentially violate laws. Pollak assured that Base is committed to promoting the distribution and exposure of quality assets and applications, acknowledging that there is significant room for improvement in this area.

data.title: Large BNB Transfer from ListaDAO to Anonymous Address
data.description: At 10:53, a significant transfer of 48,375.16 BNB was made from ListaDAO to an anonymous address, according to ChainCatcher. The transaction is valued at approximately $42.63 million. The recipient address begins with 0x04Cf.

data.title: A whale shorted over $45 million worth of SILVER on Hyperliquid with 20x leverage, and is currently experiencing a paper loss of $4.46 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale (0x61CE) shorted over $45 million worth of SILVER on Hyperliquid with 20x leverage. Two hours ago, the whale closed its position of 60,869 SILVER, worth $6.69 million, realizing a loss of $823,000. Currently, the address still holds long positions in 352,124 SILVER, worth $38.74 million, with unrealized losses reaching $4.46 million.
